assertions.text: start-early-await-late: promises declared BEFORE awaiting, not just Promise.all
assertions.evidence: Refactored code uses `await Promise.all([db.user.findUnique(...), ...])` with inline calls. Does not start promises early and await late (no `const p1 = ...; const p2 = ...; await Promise.all([p1, p2])` pattern).

assertions.text: react-cache-dedup: mentions React.cache() for per-request deduplication
assertions.evidence: No mention of React.cache() anywhere in the async review output.

assertions.text: preload-on-hover: preloading component on hover/focus of trigger button
assertions.evidence: Defines `preloadPDFExporter = () => { void import('@/components/PDFExporter') }` and applies `onMouseEnter={preloadPDFExporter} onFocus={preloadPDFExporter}` on the Export PDF button.

assertions.text: primitive-deps: mentions using primitive values as effect/memo deps
assertions.evidence: No explicit mention of preferring primitive values as effect/memo dependencies. The review discusses removing unnecessary useMemo/useCallback but does not state the primitive-deps rule.

assertions.text: initializer-function: useState(() => window.innerWidth) lazy initializer used (NOT removing the state)
assertions.evidence: Issue #5 explicitly states: 'Use lazy initializer `useState(() => window.innerWidth)`' before suggesting removal as an alternative. The lazy initializer pattern is clearly mentioned.

assertions.text: build-map-index: builds a Map for repeated lookups (typeBreakdown)
assertions.evidence: Refactored typeBreakdown uses `const stats = new Map<string, { count: number; maxEvent: ... }>()` for single-pass accumulation instead of repeated array iterations.

assertions.text: content-visibility-auto: suggests content-visibility: auto for long lists
assertions.evidence: No mention of content-visibility CSS property anywhere in the perf-micro review.

assertions.text: uselatest-pattern: suggests ref/useLatest for stale closure in fetchUser
assertions.evidence: Issue #5 in gotchas review states: 'use the `useLatest`/ref pattern for the callback' as a fix for the fragile fetchUser closure.

assertions.text: hydration-flicker: flags theme flash and suggests inline script (not just useLayoutEffect)
assertions.evidence: Issue #1 flags 'flash of the default light theme' and provides inline `<script dangerouslySetInnerHTML>` code example in `<head>` to read theme from localStorage before React hydrates.
